name: TerraUSD (UST)

custodian.name: N/A — algorithmic, no off-chain reserves at peg

regulator.name: US SEC; South Korea prosecutors; Singapore MAS

summary: Algorithmic stablecoin maintained via mint/burn arbitrage with sister token LUNA. De-pegged May 9, 2022 and collapsed within 72 hours, erasing ~$45B combined market cap. The most consequential stablecoin failure to date and the catalyst for MiCA, GENIUS Act, and HK Stablecoin Ordinance.

sources.title: SEC v. Terraform Labs and Do Kwon (Final Judgment, April 2024)
Nansen: On-chain forensics of UST de-peg

underlying_asset: None at peg. UST was backed by reflexive demand for LUNA. Luna Foundation Guard (LFG) added ~80,000 BTC reserves in Feb-Mar 2022 (~$3B at peak), fully deployed within 48 hours of de-peg without restoring confidence.
reserve_composition: Algorithmic at design (1 UST burnable for $1 of LUNA at oracle price); LFG BTC reserves exhausted during the run.
secondary_market: Previously deep liquidity on Curve (UST-3pool, '4pool'); Anchor Protocol provided 19.5% yield driving 75%+ of UST demand. All venues delisted post-collapse; USTC trades at ~$0.02 on Terra Classic.

smart_contract_audit.auditor: Cosmwasm modules audited piecemeal; no comprehensive audit of full mint/burn + Anchor + LFG system

oracle_provider: Terra validator-set oracle. Reported prices accurately during collapse — the failure was economic, not oracle-level.
redemption_mechanism: Algorithmic: 1 UST burnable for $1 of LUNA at oracle price. Designed assuming LUNA market cap > UST market cap. Failed when LUNA hyperinflated to absorb UST supply.
upgrade_authority: Terraform Labs core team controlled protocol parameters; community governance was nominal as Do Kwon and TFL held effective control.

timeline.event: Anchor Protocol launches with 19.5% UST yield
UST de-pegs to $0.985; LFG deploys BTC reserves
UST falls to $0.60; LUNA hyperinflation begins
SEC charges Terraform Labs and Do Kwon with fraud
Do Kwon extradited to United States

postmortem.root_cause: Reflexive algorithmic peg collapsed under sustained sell pressure. UST redemptions minted LUNA at oracle price, hyperinflating LUNA supply from ~350M to 6.5 trillion tokens in 72 hours. The mechanism designed to restore the peg accelerated its destruction. ~75% of UST demand was synthetic (Anchor's 19.5% yield subsidy); LFG's BTC reserves insufficient to absorb the run; Curve 3pool concentration was a single point of liquidity failure.

what_failed.issue: No exogenous collateral at peg. UST was 'backed' by reflexive demand for LUNA — collateral depended on the stablecoin's own success. Late BTC reserves were a band-aid on a fundamentally flawed design.
75%+ of demand driven by Anchor's 19.5% yield, subsidized at $4-7M/day — economically unsustainable. Liquidity concentrated in Curve 3pool; no diversified demand base.
No circuit breaker, no emergency redemption pause, no cap on LUNA minting. Governance nominally decentralized but effectively controlled by Terraform Labs.

SEC established UST and LUNA as unregistered securities; Terraform Labs made materially misleading statements about peg stability and Chai Corporation partnership. $4.5B settlement and criminal extradition followed.

postmortem.outcome: ~$45B combined market cap wiped in 72 hours. Anchor TVL fell from $14B to <$100M. Cascading contagion took down Three Arrows Capital, Celsius, Voyager within 8 weeks. Catalyzed MiCA, GENIUS Act, and HK Stablecoin Ordinance.
postmortem.rarm_lesson: UST is the canonical case for RARM's AFI (Aggregate Fragility Index) layer-gating logic: L2 Asset Quality fell below the failure threshold, so subsequent layers (L3-L6) contribute zero to the aggregate score regardless of nominal quality. No governance, technology, or market structure can compensate for collateral that is reflexive to the token it backs. Secondary lesson: L4 demand-quality flag — sustainable stablecoin demand requires organic, diversified utility, not yield arbitrage from a single source.
